title: phaseolus adenanthus var. radicans
intro: Phaseolus adenanthus var. radicans, also known as the "twining bean," is a climbing plant species belonging to the legume family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing pain and swelling, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Phaseolus adenanthus var. radicans can be made into decoctions, infusions, or ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
